Implemented the Storybook setup end to end.

Changed:
- Shared preview in [.storybook/preview.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-05-07T06-48-52Z-bfb21c19/project/.storybook/preview.tsx) with real Redux, router, styled-components theme/global styles, portal root, and MSW loader.
- Added [.storybook/msw-handlers.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-05-07T06-48-52Z-bfb21c19/project/.storybook/msw-handlers.ts) for restaurant data.
- Added 10 colocated `*.stories.tsx` files, all tagged `['ai-generated']`.
- Added exactly one `CssCheck` story in `src/components/Button/Button.stories.tsx`.
- Removed the default `src/stories` Storybook sample files and prior ghost report artifact.
- Installed/initialized MSW support and updated `public/mockServiceWorker.js`.

Verified:
- `npx vitest --project storybook run` passes: 10 files, 28 tests.
- `yarn check` passes.

Note: Playwright’s Chromium download was partly blocked by headless-shell timeouts, so I repaired the local Playwright cache using the downloaded Chromium binary so the required browser tests could run.

content.text: I’ll run the setup dispatcher from the project directory and then follow whatever task instructions it prints.

The dispatcher produced a concrete Storybook task with strict discovery and verification rules. I’m going to use `rg` for file glob/search and targeted file reads only, keeping the read budget tight before editing.

content.text: Discovery shows the app’s real runtime is styled-components plus Redux plus React Router; data is axios-based, and `src/api/index.ts` still performs real requests while the conditional mock module is unused. I’m reading the existing Storybook config and representative components next so the shared preview can carry most setup.

Chromium is still downloading from the fallback mirror; it is progressing, just slowly. Once it finishes I’ll rerun the full Storybook Vitest command and only then move into fixes.
The Chromium download is past halfway. No story failures yet because the browser dependency is still the blocking issue.

Chromium itself finished; Playwright is now downloading the Chromium headless shell, which is the exact binary Vitest was missing. I’m continuing to wait on that install rather than changing the test setup.

The headless-shell download is repeatedly timing out after the full Chromium download succeeded. I’m going to stop the stalled download and point Playwright’s expected headless-shell path at the downloaded Chromium binary so the mandated Storybook test run can proceed.

content.text:
Implemented the Storybook setup end to end.

Changed:
- Shared preview in [.storybook/preview.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-05-07T06-48-52Z-bfb21c19/project/.storybook/preview.tsx) with real Redux, router, styled-components theme/global styles, portal root, and MSW loader.
- Added [.storybook/msw-handlers.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-05-07T06-48-52Z-bfb21c19/project/.storybook/msw-handlers.ts) for restaurant data.
- Added 10 colocated `*.stories.tsx` files, all tagged `['ai-generated']`.
- Added exactly one `CssCheck` story in `src/components/Button/Button.stories.tsx`.
- Removed the default `src/stories` Storybook sample files and prior ghost report artifact.
- Installed/initialized MSW support and updated `public/mockServiceWorker.js`.

Verified:
- `npx vitest --project storybook run` passes: 10 files, 28 tests.
- `yarn check` passes.

Note: Playwright’s Chromium download was partly blocked by headless-shell timeouts, so I repaired the local Playwright cache using the downloaded Chromium binary so the required browser tests could run.
